title: Echinocereus procumbens
taxonomy: Echinocereus procumbens is a species of cactus in the genus Echinocereus. It belongs to the family Cactaceae and is further classified into the subfamily Cactoideae. This classification places it within the order Caryophyllales, which is a large group of flowering plants. The class and division classifications for Echinocereus procumbens would be Magnoliopsida and Angiosperms, respectively. Echinocereus procumbens is also sometimes referred to as a member of the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Echinocereus procumbens, also known as the trailing hedgehog or procumbent hedgehog, is a species of cactus in the genus Echinocereus. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Echinocereus procumbens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Echinocereus procumbens is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and it is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments to alleviate skin irritations and wounds.
